svg.aria-label: Tuning and stability in three parts. Left: a stability ladder - as proportional gain rises the step response goes from well-damped to oscillating at the ultimate gain Ku 0.22, the edge; a tuned point sits at about half Ku with a two-times gain margin. Centre: adding derivative raises the ultimate gain from 0.22 to 0.40, buying margin. Right: a frequency view - the control bandwidth about 4 Hz sits well left of the 12 Hz oil-spring resonance peak, the gap being the margin. Tuned gains Kp 0.15, Ki 0.4, Kd 0.01, gain margin 2.7 times, settling about 230 ms.
svg.text: Fast, but with a margin — stay back from the oscillation edge the oil spring sets.
Tuned: Kₚ=0.15, Kᵢ=0.4, K_d=0.01 — gain margin 2.7× (9 dB), settles ~230 ms, bandwidth clear of the resonance
